findings.location: s1 — 検索に使用するSQLの作成
findings.description: The SQL code example in the knowledge file omits several WHERE conditions and $sort options that are present in the source. Missing WHERE conditions: `$if(clientId) {CLIENT_ID = :clientId}`, `$if(projectType) {PROJECT_TYPE = :projectType}`, `$if(projectClass) {PROJECT_CLASS IN (:projectClass[])}`, `$if(projectEndDateBegin) {PROJECT_END_DATE >= :projectEndDateBegin}`, `$if(projectEndDateEnd) {PROJECT_END_DATE <= :projectEndDateEnd}`. Missing $sort options: `(idDesc PROJECT_ID DESC)`, `(nameDesc PROJECT_NAME DESC, PROJECT_ID DESC)`, `(endDateAsc PROJECT_END_DATE, PROJECT_ID)`, `(endDateDesc PROJECT_END_DATE DESC, PROJECT_ID DESC)`.
findings.source_evidence: Source SQL includes 8 `$if` conditions and 8 `$sort` entries; knowledge file shows only 3 conditions and 4 sort entries.

findings.location: s1 — 業務アクションの実装 (searchProjectプライベートメソッド)
findings.description: The `searchProject` private method code shown in the knowledge file omits the user context retrieval and userId assignment that appear in the source. Specifically, `LoginUserPrincipal userContext = SessionUtil.get(context, "userContext");` and `searchCondition.setUserId(userContext.getUserId());` are missing. Since `USER_ID = :userId` is a mandatory (non-`$if`) condition in the SQL, not setting userId would cause a runtime error.
findings.source_evidence: Source searchProject method: `LoginUserPrincipal userContext = SessionUtil.get(context, "userContext"); searchCondition.setUserId(userContext.getUserId());` followed by the UniversalDao call.
